near_leaf_pairs.note: Same GFICC call ('opportunity to go up in credit quality... spreads narrow... especially across the European banking capital structure', GFICC p.3); GT phrased it as higher-quality IG, model as high-quality issuers. Model row 35, GT row 60.

Same favor-the-front-end call: GFICC 'greatest opportunity... front end of government yield curves' and GAA 'leaning into shorter duration as yield curves steepen'; adjacent Duration leaves. Model row 83, GT row 56.

model_only_judgments.reason: Explicit 'risk-on stance, including an equity overweight' (GAA p.1) and Equities=OW in p.6 table; GT carried no top-level equity row.
Explicit 'Credit positioning is neutral' (GAA p.3) and Credit=N in p.6 table; GT only carried US IG Credit N.
Explicit 'we maintain a neutral stance on high yield' (GAA p.3); GT did not carry a broad HY row (its Europe HY U is a separate GFICC inference).
Explicit 'moved to short U.S. 10-year duration' (GAA p.4); GT folded this into its combined US Treasuries U row rather than a 10y-specific leaf.
Explicit 'maintain conviction in our overweights to the information technology and communication services sectors' (GAA p.4); GT carried no sector rows.
Same explicit sector-overweight sentence (GAA p.4).
Explicit funding underweight ('funded by our positions in consumer staples') plus 'consumer staples will likely be challenged' (GAA p.4).
Explicit 'inclined to limit exposure to Europe... least-favored region' (GAA p.4) and Europe ex-UK=UW in p.6 table; GT carried no regional equity rows.
'Long Gilts vs. Australian government bonds' (GAA p.4) and Australia FI=UW in p.6 table (verified).
Explicit 'long NOK (an oil exporter) vs. GBP' (GAA p.4); GT carried no FX rows.
GBP is the explicit short leg of both the NOK and AUD trades (GAA p.4).
Explicit 'long AUD (with more persistent central bank hiking bias) vs. USD and GBP' (GAA p.4).
Views-table row (Japan=OW, verified p.6 dial) plus prose 'Japan continues to stand out' (GAA p.3); GT chose not to carry GAA table rows.
Views-table row (USD=UW, verified) plus prose 'we hold onto our bearish dollar view over the medium term' (GAA p.4).
Views-table row (U.S. Large Cap=OW, verified p.6 dial); GT chose not to carry the table enumeration.
Views-table row (UK=N, verified p.6 dial); same table-enumeration rationale.
Views-table row (Australia=UW, High conviction, verified p.6 dial); same table-enumeration rationale.
Views-table row (Canada=UW, verified p.6 dial); same table-enumeration rationale.
Views-table row (Hong Kong=OW, verified p.6 dial); same table-enumeration rationale.
Views-table row (EM=OW, verified p.6 dial); same table-enumeration rationale.
Views-table row (Germany FI=UW, verified p.6 dial), corroborated by Duration description 'underweight to Bunds and Australia'.
Views-table row (Japan FI=N with positive arrow, verified p.6 dial); same table-enumeration rationale.
Views-table row (Canada FI=N, verified p.6 dial); same table-enumeration rationale.
Views-table row (Italy FI=OW, verified p.6 dial), corroborated by 'favor Gilts and BTPs'.
Views-table row (US HY=N, verified p.6 dial) plus explicit neutral-HY prose (GAA p.3).

Views-table row (EMD Sovereign=N with negative arrow, verified p.6 dial); GT expressed the same stance at the broader EM Debt - General leaf (already an exact match), so this is a finer-grained duplicate the GT omitted.
Views-table row (EUR=OW, Low conviction, verified p.6 dial); GT carried no FX rows.
Views-table row (JPY=N, verified p.6 dial); GT carried no FX rows.
Views-table row (CHF=N, verified p.6 dial); GT carried no FX rows.
Cited text (GAA p.1) is a macro base-case Brent price path ('averages around $100 in Q2 before retreating'), not an allocation view on oil; no commodity positioning exists anywhere in either doc.
GAA p.4 says only that gold's 'role as a portfolio diversifier remains intact' with factors 'to watch' — descriptive/constructive commentary with no stated overweight, so O overstates the text.

'When this period comes to an end... re-risking should focus on companies with quality balance sheets' (GAA p.4) is a conditional forward preference, not a current factor overweight; whether that counts as a live O call is a convention question.

GAA p.5 'Alpha opportunities' section says elevated dispersion 'can favor momentum-oriented factor strategies' — a hedged market-signal observation, directionally positive but arguably not a house allocation call.

Same alpha-signal section: 'Value dispersion shows a pocket of opportunity in U.S. small caps' — directionally supportive but a dispersion-signal note, not a stated small-cap overweight (and the p.6 table has no small-cap row).

gt_only_root_causes.note: GT split the GFICC front-end call into a general leaf (row 56) and a US-specific leaf; the model emitted the claim once as Duration - Short O (row 83, paired with row 56), so the underlying claim is covered under a different leaf.

Sub-leaf GT enumerated out of the securitized-credit overweight; GFICC names agency MBS only inside the scenario matrix. Model covered the claim via its exact-match Securitized/Structured O row (GT row 58).

GT inferred a neutral cash stance from 'rather than completely derisk, our bias is to remain active' (GFICC p.3); no explicit cash view exists in the source, and the model did not make the inference. Not covered elsewhere.

GT inverted 'go up in credit quality... especially across the European banking capital structure' into an explicit subordinated-debt underweight; the model emitted only the positive side (High Quality Issuers O, paired with GT Global IG Credit), so the negative leg is uncovered.

GT synthesized an underweight from the quality-upgrade bias plus ECB/BoE hike risk and 25% recession probability; nowhere stated explicitly in GFICC. Model's Global HY N / US HY N rows come from the GAA doc's explicit neutral stance — a different claim, so this is not covered.

notes: No view disagreements anywhere: all 6 exact matches and both near-leaf pairs agree, and every one of the 24 GAA views-table dial positions the model enumerated was verified correct against the rendered p.6 table. The asymmetry is structural: the model exhaustively enumerated the GAA dial table (which GT deliberately did not carry, explaining most of the 36 model_only rows), while GT went deeper on GFICC prose inference (cash, subordinated, Europe HY) that the model did not attempt. The model's only real quality issues are four soft rows (Oil, Gold, Momentum, US Small Cap/Quality) built from macro or alpha-signal commentary rather than positioning statements — three of four were already self-flagged for review by the confidence rubric.
